Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java

Interface StringValidator

All Superinterfaces:
ExtensibleObjectValidator<StringValidator,String>
All Known Implementing Classes:
StringValidatorImpl
public interface StringValidator extends ExtensibleObjectValidator<StringValidator,String>
Validates the requirements of a String.

All methods (except for asString() and those found in ExtensibleObjectValidator) imply ExtensibleObjectValidator.isNotNull().

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    BooleanValidator
    asBoolean()
    Returns a validator for the Boolean representation of the value.
    StringValidator

    Validates nested requirements.

    Validates nested requirements.
    StringValidator

    Deprecated.
    actual is already a String

    Returns a validator for the URI representation of the value.

    Validates nested requirements.

    Returns a validator for the URL representation of the value.
    StringValidator

    Validates nested requirements.
    StringValidator
    contains(String expected)
    Ensures that the actual value contains a value.
    StringValidator
    doesNotContain(String value)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain a value.
    StringValidator
    doesNotEndWith(String suffix)
    Ensures that the actual value does not end with a value.
    StringValidator
    doesNotStartWith(String prefix)
    Ensures that the actual value does not start with a value.
    StringValidator
    endsWith(String suffix)
    Ensures that the actual value ends with a value.
    StringValidator
    isBlank()
    Ensures that the actual value is empty or contains only white space codepoints.
    StringValidator
    isEmpty()
    Ensures that the actual value is empty.
    StringValidator
    isNotBlank()
    Ensures that the actual value is not empty or contains non-white space codepoints.
    StringValidator
    isNotEmpty()
    Ensures that the actual value is not empty.
    StringValidator
    isStripped()
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain leading or trailing white space, where white space is defined by strip().
    StringValidator
    isTrimmed()
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain leading or trailing white space, where white space is defined by trim().
    SizeValidator
    length()
    Returns a validator for the length of the actual value.
    StringValidator
    length(Consumer<SizeValidator> consumer)
    Validates nested requirements.
    StringValidator
    startsWith(String prefix)
    Ensures that the actual value starts with a value.
    StringValidator
    strip()
    Strips white space from the beginning and the end of the actual value, where white space is defined by the Unicode standard.
    StringValidator
    trim()
    Removes all white space from the beginning and end of the actual value, where white space is defined as any character whose codepoint is less than or equal to 'U+0020' (the space character).

    Methods declared in interface com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.extension.ExtensibleObjectValidator

    asString, getActual, getFailures, isEqualTo, isEqualTo, isInstanceOf, isNotEqualTo, isNotEqualTo, isNotInstanceOf, isNotNull, isNotOneOf, isNotSameObjectAs, isNull, isOneOf, isSameObjectAs

  • Method Details

    • startsWith

      StringValidator startsWith(String prefix)
      Ensures that the actual value starts with a value.
      Parameters:
      prefix - the value that the string must start with
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if prefix is null

    • doesNotStartWith

      StringValidator doesNotStartWith(String prefix)
      Ensures that the actual value does not start with a value.
      Parameters:
      prefix - the value that the string may not start with
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if prefix is null

    • endsWith

      StringValidator endsWith(String suffix)
      Ensures that the actual value ends with a value.
      Parameters:
      suffix - the value that the string must end with
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if suffix is null

    • doesNotEndWith

      StringValidator doesNotEndWith(String suffix)
      Ensures that the actual value does not end with a value.
      Parameters:
      suffix - the value that the string may not end with
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if suffix is null

    • contains

      StringValidator contains(String expected)
      Ensures that the actual value contains a value.
      Parameters:
      expected - the value that the string must contain
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if expected is null

    • doesNotContain

      StringValidator doesNotContain(String value)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain a value.
      Parameters:
      value - the value that the string may not contain
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if value is null

      Ensures that the actual value is empty.
      Returns:
      the updated validator

      Ensures that the actual value is not empty.
      Returns:
      the updated validator

    • isBlank

      StringValidator isBlank()
      Ensures that the actual value is empty or contains only white space codepoints.
      Returns:
      the updated validator

    • isNotBlank

      StringValidator isNotBlank()
      Ensures that the actual value is not empty or contains non-white space codepoints.
      Returns:
      the updated validator

    • length

      SizeValidator length()
      Returns a validator for the length of the actual value.
      Returns:
      a validator for the length of the actual value

    • length

      StringValidator length(Consumer<SizeValidator> consumer)
      Validates nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.
      Parameters:
      consumer - validates the length of the actual value
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null

    • trim

      StringValidator trim()
      Removes all white space from the beginning and end of the actual value, where white space is defined as any character whose codepoint is less than or equal to 'U+0020' (the space character).
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      See Also:
      • String.trim()

    • isTrimmed

      StringValidator isTrimmed()
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain leading or trailing white space, where white space is defined by trim().
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      See Also:
      • trim()
      • isBlank()

    • strip

      StringValidator strip()
      Strips white space from the beginning and the end of the actual value, where white space is defined by the Unicode standard.
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      See Also:
      • String.strip()

    • isStripped

      StringValidator isStripped()
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain leading or trailing white space, where white space is defined by strip().
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      See Also:
      • strip()
      • isBlank()

    • asInetAddress

      InetAddressValidator asInetAddress()
      Ensures that the actual value contains a valid Internet address format.
      Returns:
      a validator for the Internet address representation of the value

    • asInetAddress

      StringValidator asInetAddress(Consumer<InetAddressValidator> consumer)
      Validates nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.

      See asInetAddress() for exceptions that may be thrown to the consumer.

      Parameters:
      consumer - validates Internet addresses
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null

    • asUri

      UriValidator asUri()
      Returns a validator for the URI representation of the value.

      Technically-speaking, there is no such thing as an invalid URI format. Per RFC3986, any String can be represented as a relative URI, but Java's implementation is based on an older specification where this was not the case.

      Returns:
      a validator for the URI representation of the value
      See Also:
      • Discussion of Java URI vs RFC3986

    • asUri

      StringValidator asUri(Consumer<UriValidator> consumer)
      Validates nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.

      See asUri() for exceptions that may be thrown to the consumer.

      Parameters:
      consumer - validates URIs
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null

    • asUrl

      UrlValidator asUrl()
      Returns a validator for the URL representation of the value.
      Returns:
      a validator for the URL representation of the value

    • asUrl

      StringValidator asUrl(Consumer<UrlValidator> consumer)
      Validates nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.

      See asUrl() for exceptions that may be thrown to the consumer.

      Parameters:
      consumer - validates URLs
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null

      BooleanValidator asBoolean()
      Returns a validator for the Boolean representation of the value.
      Returns:
      a validator for the Boolean representation of the value

      • Boolean.parseBoolean(String)

    • asBoolean

      StringValidator asBoolean(Consumer<BooleanValidator> consumer)
      Validates nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.

      See asBoolean() for exceptions that may be thrown to the consumer.

      Parameters:
      consumer - validates Booleans
      Returns:
      the updated validator
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null

    • asString

      @Deprecated StringValidator asString()
      Deprecated.
      actual is already a String
      Returns a verifier for the String representation of the actual value.

      Returns:
